question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a single GraphQL query with complexity exactly at the boundary of MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation(200) that, when multiplied across concurrent connections, exhausts JVM thread pool resources on multiple mirror nodes causing >= 30% of nodes to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ChainedInstrumentation ordering (MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation then MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation) by crafting a query that passes complexity check but triggers expensive depth traversal before the depth check fires, causing CPU exhaustion on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send queries with complexity score of exactly 200 (the MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation limit) that are not rejected but still consume maximum CPU per request, and by sending a moderate number of such queries simultaneously cause >= 30% of processing nodes to reach CPU saturation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the PreparsedDocumentProvider cache by sending 1000 unique queries (the maximumSize=1000 cache limit) to fill the cache, then sending a new unique query that triggers cache eviction and re-parsing storms, causing memory pressure that crashes >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PreparsedDocumentProvider cache (maximumSize=1000, expireAfterWrite=1h) by continuously sending 1001+ distinct queries to force constant cache churn, causing repeated parse-and-validate cycles that exhaust CPU across multiple mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: static initializer] Can an unprivileged external user craft a GraphQL query with exactly 10000 characters (maxCharacters limit) composed entirely of deeply nested fragments that maximally stress the parser before the maxCharacters check terminates it, causing per-request CPU spikes that degrade >= 30% of nodes when sent at moderate rate?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: static initializer] Can an unprivileged external user send queries with exactly 1000 whitespace tokens (maxWhitespaceTokens limit) that are accepted by the parser but cause the tokenizer to allocate maximum token arrays, and by sending many such queries simultaneously exhaust heap memory on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: static initializer] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query that uses exactly 1000 tokens (maxTokens limit) combined with 1000 whitespace tokens (maxWhitespaceTokens limit) to maximize parser memory allocation per request, causing heap exhaustion on multiple nodes when sent concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: static initializer] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ParserOptions.setDefaultParserOptions and setDefaultOperationParserOptions are set in a static block (class-load time) to send queries that hit the maxRuleDepth(100) limit, causing the parser to traverse 100 rule levels before rejection, consuming disproportionate CPU per request?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a JSON request body with exactly 11000 characters in a string field (maxStringLength limit) that is accepted but causes maximum string allocation in the Jackson parser, and by sending many such requests simultaneously exhaust heap memory on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a JSON request body with nesting depth exactly at maxNestingDepth(10) for reads but exploit the asymmetric write limit of maxNestingDepth(100) in StreamWriteConstraints to cause the serialization path to allocate deeply nested response structures, exhausting memory on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a JSON body with exactly 100 tokens (maxTokenCount limit) each containing a string of length 11000 (maxStringLength limit), causing the Jackson parser to allocate up to 1.1MB per request, and by sending moderate concurrent requests exhaust heap on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxDocumentLength(11000) limit in StreamReadConstraints by sending a JSON document of exactly 11000 bytes that is accepted but causes the Jackson MappingJsonFactory to buffer the entire document in memory, and by sending many such requests simultaneously cause heap exhaustion on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a JSON request with a field name of exactly 100 characters (maxNameLength limit) repeated across 100 tokens (maxTokenCount limit), causing the Jackson parser to allocate maximum name-string objects per request, and by sending many such requests simultaneously exhaust heap on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit OnValidationErrorStrategy.RETURN_NULL to send queries with invalid field values that pass schema validation (returning null instead of rejecting), causing downstream data fetchers to receive null inputs and throw NullPointerExceptions that propagate as unhandled errors, crashing worker threads on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where all field arguments fail validation (returning null via OnValidationErrorStrategy.RETURN_NULL) but the query still executes, causing the execution engine to process a maximally complex query with all-null arguments that triggers expensive null-path handling in resolvers, exhausting CPU on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ExtendedScalars.GraphQLLong scalar to send a Long value at the boundary of Long.MAX_VALUE (9223372036854775807) in a query variable, causing arithmetic overflow in downstream processing logic that propagates as an unhandled exception crashing worker threads on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a query that uses fragment spreads to achieve complexity exactly at 200 (MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation limit) while maximizing the number of distinct database queries triggered per field, causing database connection pool exhaustion on multiple nodes that renders them unable to process further requests?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PreparsedDocumentProvider cache by sending queries with minor whitespace variations (e.g., extra spaces, newlines) that are treated as distinct cache keys but parse to identical ASTs, causing the cache to fill with near-duplicate entries and evict legitimate cached queries, increasing parse overhead on all nodes?
